data.title: Kura rises on $30 mln milestone payment after first patient dosed in late-stage trial
data.description:
** Shares of drugmaker Kura Oncology rise 4% to $9.46 in early trading

** Company gets $30 mln milestone payment after dosing first patient in a late-stage trial for acute myeloid leukemia, a fast-growing blood cancer

** The trial tests oral experimental drug ziftomenib-plus-chemo in newly diagnosed AML patients with NPM1 or KMT2A mutations - Kura

** Adds the mutations are genetic changes found in AML; NPM1 is linked to better initial chemo response, while KMT2A is tied to aggressive disease and higher relapse risk

** Trial launch announced last month; ziftomenib is a once-daily oral treatment - Kura

** The disease affects about 22,000 people in the U.S. every year, according to the American Cancer Society

** Including session's move, stock up ~7% YTD

data.title: U.S. Business Activity Rises at Fastest Pace Since July
data.description:
By Ed Frankl

U.S. business activity accelerated at the second-fastest speed this year in October, helped by increasing new business and output in both manufacturing and services.

The S&P Global Flash U.S. Composite PMI--a monthly gauge of activity--edged up to 54.8 this month from 53.9 in September. A reading above 50 suggests overall activity continued to grow, this time at the strongest pace since July.

Activity in the services sector reached a three-month high, while for manufacturing it marked a two-month high.

The survey data shows "sustained strong economic growth at the start of the fourth quarter, with business activity picking up momentum across both manufacturing and services," said Chris Williamson, chief business economist at S&P Global Market Intelligence

"However, business confidence in the outlook for the coming year has deteriorated further, and is at one of the lowest levels seen over the past three years as companies worry about the impact of policies, most notably tariffs," he added.

With the Federal Reserve expected to cut its key interest rate next week, prices charged for goods and services rose at the slowest rate since April, the survey said. Nevertheless, firms' costs continued to increase sharply, amid higher tariffs and upward wage pressures.

There were some reports of businesses being adversely affected by the government shutdown, Williamson noted.

Write to Ed Frankl at edward.frankl@wsj.com

data.title: Honeywell Intl Is Maintained at Sector Perform by RBC Capital
Consumer Sentiment Drops in October Michigan Survey
data.description:
By Matt Grossman

U.S. consumer sentiment declined in October and fell compared with an initial estimate published earlier this month, suggesting the toll from renewed trade tensions and an extended government shutdown, according to the University of Michigan's survey.

The survey index ended October at 53.6, down from an initial October reading of 55 published two weeks ago and a drop from 55.1 last month.

Write to Matt Grossman at matt.grossman@wsj.com

data.title: Canada Ready to Resume Trade Talks When U.S. Is Ready, Prime Minister Carney Says
data.description:
By Robb M. Stewart

OTTAWA--Canada stands ready to resume trade talks with the U.S. and build on recent progress to settle a dispute over tariffs that President Trump has levied on several sectors important to the Canadian economy, Prime Minister Mark Carney said.

Speaking to reporters in Ottawa before boarding a plane headed to Malaysia, Carney said that when the U.S. is ready his government would pick up where talks with counterparts in Washington where they had left off before Trump ended negotiations.

Carney said significant progress had been made during talks between the countries on specific sectors such as steel, aluminum and the automotive industry.

Trump said late Thursday he was terminating trade negotiations with Canada, pointing to a television advertisement sponsored by the Ontario government that features the voice of Ronald Reagan as he speaks negatively about tariffs. In a post on the Truth Social site, Trump on Friday accused Canada of attempting to "illegally influence" the U.S. Supreme Court with the ad as the court weighs the legality of his far-reaching tariffs

"For months we have stressed the importance of distinguishing the things we can control and the things we can't control. We can't control the trade policies of U.S.," Carney said, adding he recognize that those policies have changed significantly since the 1980s and 1990s and that the U.S. now imposes tariffs of some level on its trading partners.

Canada and the U.S. had been in discussions about how to reduce certain tariffs Trump has imposed, including the 50% levies Trump has placed on Canadian aluminum and steel. The nations are also set to begin a renegotiation of the U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement in the coming months, a review that is mandated by law to occur in 2026.

Carney said his government was focusing on areas under its control, such as areas where investment can be increased, and on helping drive efforts to find new trading partners.

Carney is scheduled to attend the Association of Southeast Asian Nations summit in Kuala Lumpur, and is set to meet with Malaysia's prime minister to discuss trade. He also has plans to meet with Singapore's prime minister and regional business leaders, and is scheduled to conclude his trip to the region in South Korea by attending a meeting of Asia-Pacific economic leaders.

Write to Robb M. Stewart at robb.stewart@wsj.com

data.title: Wall Street indexes rise with Fed rate cuts, earnings in focus
data.description:
Key points:
  • US equity indexes rise; Nasdaq out front, up ~1%
  • Comm Svcs leads S&P sector gainers; Materials off most
  • Euro STOXX 600 index edges up ~0.1%
  • Dollar ~flat; crude, bitcoin rise; gold slips
  • US 10-Year Treasury yield edges up to ~4.00%

WALL STREET INDEXES RISE WITH FED RATE CUTS, EARNINGS IN FOCUS

Wall Street's three major indexes all hit intraday record highs on Friday with rising investor confidence that the Federal Reserve will cut rates next week and again in December and with the latest earnings also lending support.

A slightly cooler than expected September reading for consumer prices supported the rate-cutting hopes and brightened the mood and kept expectations for a 25-basis-point Fed rate cut next week well above 90%, and extended hopes for another cut in December, according to CME Group's FedWatch tool.

However, the White House put a damper on the party saying that there likely will not be a release of inflation data next month due to the U.S. government shutdown. And U.S. President Trump terminated all trade talks with Canada after a Canadian political advertisement used the voice of the late president Ronald Reagan to criticize tariffs.

Meanwhile, S&P Global's flash U.S. Composite PMI Output Index showed that U.S. business activity picked up in October, but a deterioration in the economic outlook blamed on the Trump administration's protectionist trade policy limited job gains while companies sat on piles of unsold goods amid an export slump.

The Philadelphia semiconductor index is up about 2% after hitting an intraday record and outperforming the broader market.

Helping the mood is Intel , whose shares are jumping more than 2%, after it beat third-quarter profit estimates, adding some cheer in a mixed week for U.S. earnings. Earlier this week, Tesla and Netflix reported lackluster results.

Elsewhere in earnings, Ford Motor shares are up 9% after its third-quarter profit beat expectations even as it cut its profit guidance on Thursday, citing fallout from a fire at a critical aluminum supplier that will crimp production of some of its most lucrative vehicles through the end of the year.

Among the S&P 500's 11 major sectors, most are gaining with communication services leading the advance, while materials is the biggest laggard.

data.title: G&A's New Research Shows Largest U.S. Companies Continued To Adopt Sustainability Reporting as a Best Practice in 2024
data.description:
G&A's Sustainability Highlights ( 10.15.2025 )

NORTHAMPTON, MA / ACCESS Newswire / October 24, 2025 / G&A recently issued the 14th edition of our annual research report on sustainability reporting trends among the largest U.S. public companies. In our Top Story this issue, G&A's 2025 Sustainability Reporting in Focus provides detailed data and findings confirming that companies in the S&P 500 Index and the Russell 1000 index have adopted sustainability reporting as a best practice. Key takeaways from the report include:

  • A record 94% of Russell 1000 companies, making up approximately 93% of all U.S. equity market capitalization, reported on sustainability in 2024, up from 93% in 2023. This represents a significant increase from 34% reporting in the 2018 publishing year, when G&A issued our first research on the full Russell 1000.

  • The larger half by market cap of the Russell 1000 (i.e., the S&P 500) are nearing 100% reporters with a record 99% reporting on sustainability in 2024, compared to 98.6% in 2023. This compares with just 20% reporting when G&A published our first annual research on trends in the 2011 publishing year.

  • The gap between the larger half of the Russell 1000 and the smaller half by market cap (mid-cap companies with approximately $2 billion-$4 billion in market cap) continued to narrow, reaching 90% compared to 87% in 2023.

G&A's co-founders commented on the reasons behind the increased adoption of sustainability reporting as a best practice for the largest U.S. companies. Louis Coppola, G&A's Chief Executive Officer, said, "Our Trends research over the past 14 years demonstrates how U.S. companies adopted sustainability reporting as a best practice because stakeholders demanded it, not because regulators required it. While policies may shift in Washington or Brussels, the fundamental reasons for sustainability reporting do not change: the reporting process helps leaders sharpen strategy, strengthen resilience, understand risk, allocate capital, create value and build trust."

Hank Boerner, G&A's Chairman and Chief Strategist, said, "At the heart of sustainability disclosure is a better understanding of risk and reward - something investors and stakeholders deeply appreciate. Despite the anti-ESG pushback in some quarters, Corporate America continues to innovate and push forward with more detailed and informative reporting on a widening range of topics. Companies on the leading edge of this trend are well positioned for upcoming shifts from voluntary to mandatory sustainability reporting in a growing number of jurisdictions."

G&A's 2025 Sustainability Reporting in Focus report provides analysis of corporate report content including reporting frameworks and standards used - such as GRI, SASB, and TCFD. SASB continued to be the most widely used sustainability standard in 2024, with 82% of Russell 1000 reporters aligning with SASB, compared with 55% aligning with GRI. up from 78% in 2022. TCFD reporting continued to increase, with 65% of Russell 1000 reporters aligning with TCFD in 2024, compared to 60% in 2023.

Our research also examines alignment with initiatives such as the UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), trends in external assurance, and CDP reporting. For the first time, we began tracking alignment with new reporting initiatives: the International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) Sustainability Standards, the European Sustainability Reporting Standards (ESRS), and the Task Force for Nature-related Disclosures (TNFD). The report also includes sector-specific analysis of reporting trends within all 11 sectors of the Global Industry Classification Standard (GICS®).

As always, the G&A team is available to discuss our research and stands ready to help your company navigate the shifting to provide counsel to help your company prepare for upcoming shifts from voluntary to mandatory sustainability reporting in a growing number of jurisdictions. Reach out to us at: info@ga-institute.com.

data.title: Alaska Air Facing Another Cost Concern with Latest Outage — Market Talk
data.description: Alaska Air Group may be facing another million-dollar cost headwind from a technology outage on Thursday that forced it to ground flights systemwide. The carrier says the outage was tied to a failure at its primary data center and that the ground stop caused delays and cancellations. Alaska faced a similar outage just three months ago when a critical piece of hardware at a data center failed, bringing down its systems and forcing it to cancel about 10% of its mainline schedule. The airline later said that the ground stop hurt its earnings by 10 cents a share. Based on the 117.5 million shares it had outstanding as of Sept. 30, that would translate to an earnings impact of about $11.75 million. Alaska cited the outage as a primary reason that unit costs were up 8.6% in 3Q. Shares fall 4.4%. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

data.title: TSX climbs as US inflation data boosts rate-cut hopes
data.description:
By Ragini Mathur

Canada's main stock index climbed on Friday after a softer-than-expected U.S. inflation report strengthened bets for Federal Reserve interest rate cuts, while investors appeared undeterred by emerging trade frictions between the U.S. and Canada.

At 10:11 a.m. ET (14xx GMT), Toronto's S&P/TSX composite index was up 0.7% at 30,384.11 points, on track to record its second straight weekly advance.

Wall Street indexes were also higher after data showed U.S. inflation rose less than expected last month, keeping the Fed on track to cut interest rates next week.

At home, the Bank of Canada is also set to announce its monetary policy decision on October 29 and markets are currently pricing in about 94% chances of a 25-basis-point easing, swap market data showed.

"The big picture is we have the (Fed and Bank of Canada) meetings next week on the same day, it's very likely we'll get rate cuts on both sides of the border and I think any of today's news hasn't impacted that calculus on the part of the central bankers," said Brian Madden, chief investment officer at First Avenue Investment Counsel Inc.

The technology sector led the TSX's advance both for the day and week, posting 4.7% gains. Cryptocurrency miner Bitfarms saw its shares soar 12.9% after trading firm Jane Street revealed a 5% ownership stake in the company.

Heavyweight financials (.SPTTFS) advanced 0.5%, while consumer discretionary stocks (.GSPTTCD) rose 0.4%.

Investors appeared to be unfazed by the uncertainties stemming from U.S. President Donald Trump's announcement late Thursday that he had terminated all trade discussions with Canada after what he described as a "fraudulent advertisement" featuring former President Ronald Reagan criticizing tariffs.

"Trade talks are off today, they might get on tomorrow... I think investors are understanding this is a part of how the President operates and so they're not too fussed," Madden said.

Materials , which recorded the week's worst performance with a 4.5% decline, gained 0.5% on Friday. Meanwhile, energy stocks were up 3.5% for the week and edged up 0.1% during the session.

data.title: India's RBI proposes limits for banks' capital market exposure, acquisition funding
data.description:
The Reserve Bank of India released a draft circular on Friday, proposing that banks' total direct capital market and acquisition finance exposures must not exceed 20% of their tier 1 capital.

The RBI also proposed that the aggregate capital market exposure of banks to not exceed 40% of their tier 1 capital.

Tier 1 capital, the highest-quality capital of a bank, includes equity, retained earnings, and certain instruments that can absorb losses.

Earlier this month, the regulator allowed banks to fund acquisitions and raised the cap on loans for buying shares at initial public offerings, as part of a raft of measures to boost bank lending in the world's fifth-largest economy.

RBI proposed that the aggregate exposure of a bank towards acquisition finance shall not exceed 10% of its tier 1 capital.

In its proposed rules for acquisition finance, it said that banks may finance a maximum of 70% of the deal value, with at least 30% to be funded by the acquiring company.

It added that commercial banks can offer acquisition finance only to listed entities that have a satisfactory net worth and been profitable for the last three years.

data.title: Major shareholder notification
data.description:
OSLO, Norway - October 24, 2025 - Agilyx ASA (OSE: AGLX: OTCQX: AGXXF: ISIN:NO0010872468: WKN: A2QGQ) ("Agilyx" or the "Company") announces CirCap MasterIS.à.r.l ("CirCap Master") has today transferred all its 7,029,469 shares inAgilyx ASA (the "Company") that it received as consideration in relation totheclosing of the GreenDot Global share acquisition as announced by the Companyon15 October 2025, to its parent company, Circap Funds SCA SICAV RAIF ("CirCapFunds"). Following completion of the transaction, CirCap Master no longerholdsany shares in the Company, and Circap Funds owns 7,029,469 shares in theCompany, corresponding to an ownership interest of approximately 5.61% of theCompany's 125,352,758 outstanding shares and votes. The shares remain subjecttoa lock-up undertaking expiring 10 January 2026, followed by a 90-day windowduring which any sale of the consideration shares received by the sellers intheGreenDot Global acquisition will be capped at 20% of the prior 30-day averagedaily trading volume in the Company's shares.

This information is subject to the disclosure requirements pursuant tosections4-2 and 5 -12 of the Norwegian Securities Trading Act.

ABOUT AGILYXAgilyx ASA is a leading global investment platform supporting the developmentofplastic waste feedstock supply to the recycling industry. Through its 44%stakein GreenDot Global, Europe's largest waste plastic recycling platform,generating over EUR 400 million in annual revenues, Agilyx gains access tolargevolumes of post-use plastic and advanced sorting and recycling infrastructureinGermany, Austria, and Italy, helping supply critical European-sourcedfeedstockto the European advanced recycling markets. Through Cyclyx, its (50%) jointventure with ExxonMobil (25%) and LyondellBasell (25%), Agilyx supports thecollection and processing of post-use plastic waste into custom-formulated,high-quality feedstock solutions for global plastic producers. Agilyx marketsStyrenyx, its proprietary advanced recycling technology, to recyclepolystyrenewaste into recycled styrene monomers for reuse in new, high-quality products.Byenabling the shift from a linear "make-take-waste" model to a circulareconomy,Agilyx helps solve the crisis of plastic waste and supports the globaltransition to a low-carbon future.

Inquiries: investors@agilyx.comLearn more at: www.agilyx.comFor media resources, visit: https://www.agilyx.com/media-room/
